articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has invested in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The deal signals growing institutional investor interest in FEC-focused fintech infrastructure. For FEC operators, this investment could accelerate product development and broader adoption of cashless and integrated payment solutions across the sector.

articles.title: Sacoa Installs Cashless Technology at Virginia Pinstack Location
articles.summary: Amusement industry payments leader Sacoa has completed a cashless technology installation at a Pinstack family entertainment center in Virginia. The deployment highlights the continued push toward fully integrated cashless play systems in multi-attraction FEC venues. Operators considering similar upgrades can look to this as a real-world case study in modernizing payment infrastructure.

articles.title: Six Flags Fiesta Texas Unveils Werewolf Gorge, World's Longest Family Launch Coaster
articles.summary: Six Flags Fiesta Texas has announced Werewolf Gorge, a record-breaking family launch roller coaster featuring four launches, a mid-course rollback, and immersive storytelling elements with a 39-inch height requirement. The attraction is designed to appeal across multiple generations, a strategy increasingly relevant for FEC operators seeking to broaden their audience. The announcement underscores the industry trend of combining narrative theming with family-accessible thrill rides.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Names Josh Cole as First Chief Marketing Officer
articles.summary: Indoor slide and action park chain Slick City has appointed Josh Cole as its first-ever CMO as the brand accelerates expansion to 40-plus locations across North America and internationally. The hire signals a maturation of the Slick City brand and a more sophisticated marketing push to match its rapid growth. FEC operators and investors should watch Slick City as a benchmark for scaling a differentiated indoor attraction concept.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Track in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton featuring a multi-level go-kart track, continuing the brand's national expansion. The multi-level format maximizes floor space in urban and suburban retail environments, a design approach with broad applicability for FEC developers. This opening reinforces indoor karting as one of the fastest-growing FEC attraction categories.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Parks Expands into Maryland with New Indoor Entertainment Venue
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Parks continues its aggressive national growth strategy with a new Maryland location, adding to its extensive portfolio of indoor trampoline and adventure park venues. The brand's consistent expansion highlights strong consumer demand for multi-attraction indoor entertainment destinations. FEC operators should note Urban Air's market penetration strategy as a case study in franchise-driven growth.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Closes Permanently After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has shut down after only two years of operation, highlighting the challenges facing newer FEC entrants in competitive or underserved markets. The closure follows a pattern of post-pandemic FEC openings that struggled to achieve sustainable revenue. Operators and investors should examine the factors behind early-stage FEC failures as cautionary data points for site selection and financial planning.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Asmodee for Spot It! Summer Experience
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has teamed with board game publisher Asmodee to integrate the popular Spot It! game into a summer in-venue experience. The partnership demonstrates a broader trend of FEC brands collaborating with IP holders to create fresh, themed programming that drives repeat visits. For FEC operators, this type of limited-time branded event offers a low-cost way to refresh guest experiences and generate marketing buzz.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Signs Lease in Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease to occupy part of a former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the trend of FECs backfilling large vacant retail footprints. Repurposing dead mall anchors remains one of the most significant real estate opportunities for FEC expansion. This deal is a useful reference point for operators and developers evaluating similar adaptive-reuse opportunities.

articles.title: Slick City Indoor Slide Park Expanding to Milford This Summer
articles.summary: Slick City is bringing its proprietary dry-slide indoor action park concept to Milford, adding another location to its rapidly growing portfolio. The brand's consistent geographic expansion demonstrates strong franchise demand and consumer appetite for novel indoor attractions beyond traditional bowling and arcade formats. FEC operators should monitor Slick City's growth trajectory as it increasingly competes for the same suburban family demographic.

articles.title: Private Equity Firm Periscope Invests in Amusement Connect Platform
articles.summary: Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a payments and management platform tailored to the amusement and FEC industry. The backing of a dedicated PE firm lends significant financial resources and growth credibility to the platform. FEC suppliers and operators using or evaluating Amusement Connect should anticipate accelerated feature development and potential expansion of its service footprint.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is slated to open in Mt. Juliet, a fast-growing Nashville suburb, signaling continued developer confidence in FEC concepts in Sun Belt growth markets. Mt. Juliet's expanding residential base makes it an attractive target for family-focused entertainment venues. The announcement is a useful data point for operators and investors tracking new FEC development activity.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center Opens in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new location in Carrollton, Georgia, expanding the brand's presence in the Southeast. New FEC openings in secondary markets reflect growing confidence in consumer demand outside major metropolitan areas. Operators and suppliers should note the continued geographic diversification of FEC development beyond Tier 1 cities.

articles.title: Madison's 'The Gallery' Mixed-Use Entertainment District to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison this summer, featuring a mix of food, beverage, and entertainment concepts. Large-format mixed-use entertainment destinations are increasingly competing for the same consumer occasions as standalone FECs. Operators should watch this format trend as it reshapes how families and adults choose entertainment outings.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Opens Second Dubai Venue, Expanding Indoor Golf Internationally
articles.summary: Five Iron Golf has opened its second location in Dubai's Business Bay district, marking a significant step in the brand's international expansion. The move underscores the global appeal of tech-driven indoor golf entertainment as a premium FEC attraction. Operators in the golf entertainment space should note Five Iron's international playbook as the indoor golf segment matures beyond its North American roots.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Fire-Breathing Dragon Centerpiece
articles.summary: A new resort called Dellshire has opened in Wisconsin Dells, featuring a dramatic fire-breathing dragon as its central entertainment attraction. The property adds to the competitive entertainment landscape in one of the Midwest's most concentrated family tourism markets. FEC operators can look at Dellshire's theatrical approach to venue differentiation as an example of experience-led design driving destination appeal.

articles.title: Hasbro Launches AI Studio Sixth Wall to Power Licensed Character Experiences in LBE
articles.summary: Hasbro has created Sixth Wall, an AI studio built around its CharacterOS system, enabling licensed use of Hasbro characters in location-based entertainment, interactive storytelling, and digital companions. The platform ensures character authenticity through authorized voice performances and safety guardrails, making it a credible option for FEC operators seeking branded IP partnerships. This development could open new licensing pathways for FECs looking to add recognizable character experiences without complex custom development.

articles.title: London-Style Interactive Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment space combining foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is expanding from London to Paris, reflecting the growing international popularity of social gaming venues. This format blends FEC-style gaming with F&B in a format targeted at young adults, a segment increasingly pursued by FEC operators. The Paris expansion highlights how European markets are adopting entertainment models that North American FECs have pioneered.

articles.title: H. Betti Industries Appoints Michael Arnone as Senior Marketing Director
articles.summary: Amusement industry distributor H. Betti Industries has named Michael Arnone as Senior Director of Marketing, bringing over 20 years of brand strategy and digital marketing experience to the role. The appointment signals a push to strengthen H. Betti's market presence and customer engagement at a time of growing competition among amusement equipment distributors. FEC operators who work with H. Betti can expect a more robust marketing and support apparatus from the company.

articles.title: Disguise to Debut New Projection Mapping and Immersive Visual Tools at InfoComm 2026
articles.summary: Media server company Disguise will unveil a new version of its Mapping Matter projection mapping application at InfoComm 2026, featuring an expanded projector library and improved project planning tools. The platform is used in immersive attractions and location-based entertainment installations, making it directly relevant for FEC operators investing in projection-based experiences. Attendees at InfoComm can see live demonstrations of workflows designed to simplify complex multi-projector setups.

articles.title: OKANA Resort Fully Open for First Summer Season in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: OKANA Resort in Oklahoma City has opened fully for its inaugural summer season, positioning itself as a major new family entertainment destination in the region. The property adds significant competition to the regional family entertainment and hospitality market. FEC operators in the area should assess how a full-service resort with built-in entertainment affects their customer acquisition strategies.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns This Summer at Kentucky and Southern Indiana Venues
articles.summary: Bowling centers in Kentucky and Southern Indiana are again participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary games to children to drive traffic during the traditionally slower summer months. The program remains one of the most effective community-engagement tools available to bowling FEC operators for building family loyalty and introducing new customers. Operators not yet enrolled in similar promotions should consider the program's value as a low-cost guest acquisition strategy.

articles.title: Play CENLA Family Entertainment Center Shuts Down in Louisiana
articles.summary: Play CENLA, a family entertainment center in Rapides Parish, Louisiana, has permanently closed its doors. The closure adds to a string of FEC shutdowns that reflect the ongoing challenges of sustaining smaller, independent venues in competitive leisure markets. Operators of similarly sized independent FECs should review their cost structures and revenue diversification strategies in light of these closures.

articles.title: Brogent Technologies Opens R&D and Manufacturing Facility in Kaohsiung, Taiwan
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies, a leading maker of media-based rides and immersive attraction systems, has inaugurated a new facility in Kaohsiung's Ciaotou Science Park equipped with automated production and AI-powered simulation technology. The expansion is designed to accelerate development cycles and increase global production capacity for flying theaters and motion attractions. FEC operators and developers sourcing large-format immersive rides should take note of Brogent's enhanced ability to deliver projects faster and at scale.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Space Transforms Mall Anchor Into Experiential Destination
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment concept is generating attention for its ability to create a distinct atmosphere inside a traditional mall setting, drawing foot traffic back to retail centers. The model aligns with the growing strategy of converting underperforming mall space into experiential FEC-style venues. Operators and real estate partners evaluating adaptive-reuse opportunities should consider this as a template for mall-based entertainment development.

articles.title: Kennywood Activates Chaperone Policy Following Rumors of Large Gathering
articles.summary: Kennywood amusement park has put its chaperone policy into effect following social media rumors of an organized group gathering at the park. Chaperone and guest conduct policies have become an increasingly important operational and safety tool for parks and FECs managing large youth crowds. Operators should review their own policies and communication protocols in light of how quickly social-media-driven events can affect venue operations.

articles.title: Wild Waves Theme Park to Close After Nearly 50 Years
articles.summary: Wild Waves Theme Park in the Pacific Northwest has announced it will permanently close after nearly five decades of operation, marking one of the most significant regional amusement park closures in recent memory. The shuttering of a long-established venue highlights how changing demographics, rising operational costs, and competition from newer FEC formats can erode the viability of legacy parks. Operators and investors should view this closure as a prompt to assess long-term positioning and capital reinvestment strategies.

articles.title: Ninja Kingdom Indoor Play Attraction Launches at Delhi Mall This Summer
articles.summary: Nexus Select CITYWALK has brought Ninja Kingdom, a ninja-themed indoor entertainment experience, to a major Delhi shopping mall for the summer season. The activation reflects the growing trend of seasonal pop-up FEC concepts in retail environments across Asian markets. Operators exploring seasonal or pop-up attraction strategies can look to this model as a low-risk way to test new concepts and locations.

articles.title: Idlewild & SoakZone Launches New Spookley's Summer Celebration Event Series
articles.summary: Idlewild & SoakZone has introduced a new family event, Spookley's Summer Celebration, running weekends in June and featuring character experiences, dance parties, and interactive activities. The event demonstrates how parks can leverage existing character IP to build engaging seasonal programming that drives repeat weekend visits. FEC operators looking to add structured event programming should note the format as a scalable model for family-focused seasonal activation.
